name: One Aspect — the smallest casting
description: The minimal casting, and the purest statement of this channel's rule: one position, filled by ANY single aspect of your own chart — a planet in its sign, a house and what stands in it, or an aspect between two planets. You choose it (or a chart calculation surfaces the day's exact aspect); it is never drawn at random, because the deck being 'drawn' from is your own chart. Read against the flagship interpretation set (western-astrology-canonical) or any voice library. A chart is a mirror and a calendar, never a command.

casting._note: draw:'choose' — the querent picks one feature of their own chart (no birth data required if they already know a placement; a full chart calc can instead offer today's exact transits to choose from). Resolution: the chosen feature binds to its matching item(s) in the `from` grammar — a planet item + sign item, a house item, or an aspect item + the two planet items it joins.

sections.Position: One feature of your own chart, chosen by you: a planet in its sign (your Mercury in Gemini), a house and its tenants (your 10th and what stands there), or an aspect between two planets (your Moon square Saturn). The whole casting is this single mirror.
sections.Prompt: Sit with the one item (or pair) this feature resolves to. Ask three questions the tradition would ask, in order: What does this describe that I recognize? What does it describe that I resist? And what would relating to it — not obeying it — change this week? One aspect, honestly read, beats a whole chart skimmed.
